.github/workflows/benchmark.yml:
name: HPC Optimizer Benchmark (Performance Regression)

on:
  schedule:
    # Run nightly at 02:00 UTC on weekdays
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1-5'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      param_scale:
        description: 'Parameter scale for benchmark (millions)'
        required: false
        default: '100'
        type: string
      world_size:
        description: 'Number of GPUs for distributed benchmarks'
        required: false
        default: '1'
        type: string

concurrency:
  group: benchmark-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  BENCHMARK_THRESHOLD_PCT: "10"   # Fail if regression > 10% vs baseline

jobs:
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 1: Optimizer throughput benchmarks (single GPU)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  benchmark-single-gpu:
    name: Optimizer Throughput Benchmark
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu]
    timeout-minutes: 60

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0   # Need history for baseline comparison

      - name: Build Release (sm_80)
        run: |
          cmake -B build_bench \
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCUDA_ARCHS="80" \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON \
            -DCMAKE_CUDA_FLAGS="-O3 --use_fast_math"
          cmake --build build_bench --parallel $(nproc)

      - name: Warm up GPU
        run: |
          # Run a short warm-up to ensure GPU is at boost clock
          build_bench/examples/train_single --steps 20 --params 10000000 \
            2>/dev/null || true

      - name: Run optimizer throughput benchmark
        run: |
          PARAM_M=${{ github.event.inputs.param_scale || '100' }}
          N=$((PARAM_M * 1000000))

          echo "=== HPC Optimizer Benchmark ===" | tee benchmark_results.txt
          echo "Date: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> benchmark_results.txt
          echo "GPU: $(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader | head -1)" >> benchmark_results.txt
          echo "Params: ${PARAM_M}M (${N} elements)" >> benchmark_results.txt
          echo "" >> benchmark_results.txt

          # Run training suite benchmark (outputs timing per optimizer)
          build_bench/examples/train_single \
            --steps 200 \
            --params ${N} \
            --benchmark-mode \
            2>&1 | tee -a benchmark_results.txt

      - name: Run ZeRO-2 vs ZeRO-0 benchmark
        run: |
          echo "" >> benchmark_results.txt
          echo "=== ZeRO-2 vs ZeRO-0 Benchmark ===" >> benchmark_results.txt
          build_bench/examples/train_zero2 \
            --steps 100 \
            --params 64000000 \
            --benchmark-mode \
            2>&1 | tee -a benchmark_results.txt

      - name: Check GPU utilization
        run: |
          echo "" >> benchmark_results.txt
          echo "=== GPU Stats ===" >> benchmark_results.txt
          nvidia-smi --query-gpu=utilization.gpu,utilization.memory,memory.used,memory.free,clocks.gr \
            --format=csv,noheader,nounits >> benchmark_results.txt

      - name: Store baseline (main branch only)
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          cp benchmark_results.txt benchmark_baseline.txt
          echo "COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> benchmark_baseline.txt

      - name: Compare against baseline
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          if [ -f benchmark_baseline.txt ]; then
            echo "Comparing against stored baseline..."
            python3 scripts/check_regression.py \
              --current benchmark_results.txt \
              --baseline benchmark_baseline.txt \
              --threshold ${BENCHMARK_THRESHOLD_PCT}
          else
            echo "No baseline found — skipping regression check"
          fi

      - name: Upload benchmark results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results-${{ github.sha }}
          path: |
            benchmark_results.txt
            benchmark_baseline.txt
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Post benchmark summary to PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: marocchino/sticky-pull-request-comment@v2
        with:
          header: benchmark
          message: |
            ## 🚀 Benchmark Results

            <details>
            <summary>Click to expand throughput numbers</summary>

            ```
            $(cat benchmark_results.txt)
            ```
            </details>

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 2: Multi-GPU scaling benchmark (2/4/8 GPUs)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  benchmark-scaling:
    name: Distributed Scaling Benchmark (${{ matrix.world_size }}× GPU)
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu, multi-gpu]
    timeout-minutes: 90
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'

    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        world_size: [2, 4, 8]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build Release
        run: |
          cmake -B build_bench \
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCUDA_ARCHS="80" \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI=ON \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON
          cmake --build build_bench --parallel $(nproc)

      - name: Check GPU availability
        id: gpu_check
        run: |
          GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader | wc -l)
          echo "gpu_count=${GPU_COUNT}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Available GPUs: ${GPU_COUNT}"

      - name: Run ZeRO-2 scaling (${{ matrix.world_size }}× GPU)
        if: steps.gpu_check.outputs.gpu_count >= matrix.world_size
        run: |
          WS=${{ matrix.world_size }}
          echo "=== ZeRO-2 Scaling: ${WS}× GPU ===" | tee scaling_${WS}gpu.txt
          mpirun --allow-run-as-root -np ${WS} \
            --bind-to none \
            -x NCCL_DEBUG=WARN \
            -x NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0 \
            build_bench/examples/train_zero2 \
              --steps 50 \
              --params 500000000 \
              --benchmark-mode \
            2>&1 | tee -a scaling_${WS}gpu.txt

      - name: Upload scaling results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: scaling-benchmark-${{ matrix.world_size }}gpu-${{ github.sha }}
          path: scaling_*.txt
          retention-days: 90

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 3: Memory footprint audit
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  benchmark-memory:
    name: Memory Footprint Audit
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu]
    timeout-minutes: 30
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build Release
        run: |
          cmake -B build_bench \
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCUDA_ARCHS="80" \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON
          cmake --build build_bench --parallel $(nproc)

      - name: Run memory audit (cuda-memcheck / compute-sanitizer)
        run: |
          echo "=== Memory Audit ===" | tee memory_audit.txt
          compute-sanitizer --tool memcheck \
            build_bench/tests/test_hpc_optimizers \
            2>&1 | grep -E "(ERROR|LEAK|bytes|OK)" | tee -a memory_audit.txt || true

          echo "" >> memory_audit.txt
          echo "=== ZeRO Memory Footprint ===" >> memory_audit.txt
          compute-sanitizer --tool memcheck \
            build_bench/tests/test_zero2 \
            2>&1 | grep -E "(ERROR|LEAK|bytes|OK)" | tee -a memory_audit.txt || true

      - name: Upload memory audit
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: memory-audit-${{ github.sha }}
          path: memory_audit.txt
          retention-days: 30
.github/workflows/ci.yml:
name: HPC Optimizer CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      run_multigpu:
        description: 'Run multi-GPU tests (requires 2+ GPUs on runner)'
        required: false
        default: 'false'
        type: boolean

# Cancel in-progress runs for the same branch/PR
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  CUDA_VERSION: "12.2"
  NCCL_VERSION: "2.18"
  CMAKE_VERSION: "3.27"

jobs:
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 1: Build matrix (Debug + Release, sm_80)
  # Runs on self-hosted SLURM runner with GPU node
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build (${{ matrix.build_type }}, sm_${{ matrix.sm }})
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu]
    timeout-minutes: 30

    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        build_type: [Release, Debug]
        sm: ["80"]          # Primary: A100
        include:
          - build_type: Release
            sm: "70"        # V100 compat check (compile only, no runtime test)
          - build_type: Release
            sm: "90"        # H100 compat check (compile only, no runtime test)

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Verify CUDA environment
        run: |
          nvcc --version
          nvidia-smi
          echo "CUDA_HOME=${CUDA_HOME}"
          echo "LD_LIBRARY_PATH=${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"

      - name: Cache CMake build directory
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: build_${{ matrix.build_type }}_sm${{ matrix.sm }}
          key: cmake-${{ matrix.build_type }}-sm${{ matrix.sm }}-${{ hashFiles('CMakeLists.txt', 'include/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            cmake-${{ matrix.build_type }}-sm${{ matrix.sm }}-

      - name: Configure CMake
        run: |
          cmake -B build_${{ matrix.build_type }}_sm${{ matrix.sm }} \
            -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=${{ matrix.build_type }} \
            -DCUDA_ARCHS="${{ matrix.sm }}" \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI=ON \
            -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON \
            -DCMAKE_CUDA_FLAGS="-Xptxas -v" \
            -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILE=OFF \
            2>&1 | tee cmake_configure_${{ matrix.build_type }}_sm${{ matrix.sm }}.log

      - name: Build
        run: |
          cmake --build build_${{ matrix.build_type }}_sm${{ matrix.sm }} \
            --parallel $(nproc) \
            2>&1 | tee cmake_build_${{ matrix.build_type }}_sm${{ matrix.sm }}.log

      - name: Upload build logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-logs-${{ matrix.build_type }}-sm${{ matrix.sm }}
          path: |
            cmake_configure_*.log
            cmake_build_*.log
          retention-days: 7

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 2: Unit + integration tests (Release, sm_80 only)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Tests (${{ matrix.suite }})
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu]
    needs: build
    timeout-minutes: 60

    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        suite:
          - test_hpc_optimizers   # 12 tests: analytic, convergence, throughput
          - test_precision         # 12 tests: FP32/FP16/BF16 round-trips, NaN
          - test_zero2             # 12 tests: ZeRO-2 sharding, convergence, 500M tput
          - test_zero3             # 12 tests: ZeRO-3 lifecycle, grad clip, memory

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Restore build cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: build_Release_sm80
          key: cmake-Release-sm80-${{ hashFiles('CMakeLists.txt', 'include/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            cmake-Release-sm80-

      - name: Rebuild if cache miss
        run: |
          if [ ! -f "build_Release_sm80/CMakeCache.txt" ]; then
            cmake -B build_Release_sm80 \
              -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
              -DCUDA_ARCHS="80" \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI=ON \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON
            cmake --build build_Release_sm80 --parallel $(nproc)
          fi

      - name: Set GPU environment
        run: |
          # Pin to a single GPU for unit tests (no NCCL needed)
          echo "CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Run ${{ matrix.suite }}
        working-directory: build_Release_sm80
        run: |
          ctest \
            --test-dir . \
            --output-on-failure \
            --timeout 300 \
            -R "${{ matrix.suite }}" \
            --output-junit ../test_results_${{ matrix.suite }}.xml \
            -V 2>&1 | tee ../test_output_${{ matrix.suite }}.log

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results-${{ matrix.suite }}
          path: |
            test_results_${{ matrix.suite }}.xml
            test_output_${{ matrix.suite }}.log
          retention-days: 14

      - name: Publish test report
        if: always()
        uses: mikepenz/action-junit-report@v4
        with:
          report_paths: test_results_${{ matrix.suite }}.xml
          check_name: "Test Report — ${{ matrix.suite }}"
          fail_on_failure: true
          require_tests: true

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 3: Multi-GPU tests (2-GPU NCCL, only if ≥2 GPUs)
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test-multigpu:
    name: Multi-GPU Tests (NCCL, 2× GPU)
    runs-on: [self-hosted, slurm, gpu, multi-gpu]
    needs: [build, test]
    timeout-minutes: 45
    # Only run on push to main OR manual trigger with flag set
    if: |
      (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main') ||
      (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.run_multigpu == 'true')

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Restore build cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: build_Release_sm80
          key: cmake-Release-sm80-${{ hashFiles('CMakeLists.txt', 'include/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            cmake-Release-sm80-

      - name: Rebuild if cache miss
        run: |
          if [ ! -f "build_Release_sm80/CMakeCache.txt" ]; then
            cmake -B build_Release_sm80 \
              -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
              -DCUDA_ARCHS="80" \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI=ON \
              -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON
            cmake --build build_Release_sm80 --parallel $(nproc)
          fi

      - name: Check GPU count
        id: gpu_check
        run: |
          GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader | wc -l)
          echo "gpu_count=${GPU_COUNT}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Found ${GPU_COUNT} GPU(s)"
          if [ "${GPU_COUNT}" -lt 2 ]; then
            echo "Skipping multi-GPU tests: fewer than 2 GPUs available"
          fi

      - name: Run multi-GPU example (2 ranks, NCCL)
        if: steps.gpu_check.outputs.gpu_count >= 2
        run: |
          # Launch train_multigpu with torchrun-style launcher via mpirun
          mpirun --allow-run-as-root -np 2 \
            --bind-to none \
            --map-by slot:PE=16 \
            -x NCCL_DEBUG=WARN \
            -x NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0 \
            -x CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID \
            build_Release_sm80/examples/train_multigpu \
              --steps 100 \
              --params 64000000 \
            2>&1 | tee multigpu_test.log

      - name: Upload multi-GPU logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: multigpu-test-logs
          path: multigpu_test.log
          retention-days: 7

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 4: Aggregate result gate
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────
  ci-gate:
    name: CI Gate (all tests pass)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, test]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check all jobs passed
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ needs.build.result }}" != "success" ]]; then
            echo "Build job failed"; exit 1
          fi
          if [[ "${{ needs.test.result }}" != "success" ]]; then
            echo "Test job failed"; exit 1
          fi
          echo "All required jobs passed ✓"
CMakeLists.txt:
# =============================================================================
# HPC CUDA Optimizer Library
# CMakeLists.txt  –  HPC build system
#
# Targets:  V100 (sm_70) | A100 (sm_80) | H100 (sm_90) | RTX 4090 (sm_89)
# CUDA 12.x | C++17 | Optional: MPI, NCCL, NVTX
#
# Usage:
#   mkdir build && cd build
#   cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
#            -DCUDA_ARCHS="70;80;90"         # multi-arch fat binary
#            -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL=ON            # link NCCL for multi-GPU
#            -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI=ON             # MPI bootstrap
#            -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX=ON            # Nsight profiling markers
#   make -j$(nproc)
#   ctest --output-on-failure
# =============================================================================
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.22)
project(HPC_Optimizer LANGUAGES CXX CUDA)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standards
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD          17)
set(CMAKE_CUDA_STANDARD         17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_CUDA_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

if(NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
  set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Release)
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GPU architecture selection
#   Default: sm_70 + sm_80 + sm_90 fat binary (covers V100→H100)
#   Override: -DCUDA_ARCHS="80;90"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(NOT DEFINED CUDA_ARCHS)
  set(CUDA_ARCHS "70;80;90")
endif()
set(CMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES ${CUDA_ARCHS})

message(STATUS "[HPC_Optimizer] GPU architectures: ${CUDA_ARCHS}")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional features
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
option(HPC_ENABLE_NCCL "Enable NCCL multi-GPU communication" OFF)
option(HPC_ENABLE_MPI  "Enable MPI for multi-node bootstrap"  OFF)
option(HPC_ENABLE_NVTX "Enable NVTX profiling markers"        OFF)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Global CUDA compilation flags
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
set(HPC_CUDA_FLAGS
    --use_fast_math                 # reciprocal sqrt, fma, etc.
    -Xcompiler=-Wall
    -Xcompiler=-Wextra
    --expt-relaxed-constexpr        # constexpr in device lambdas
    --extended-lambda               # device lambdas
    --generate-line-info            # source correlation in Nsight
    -Xcompiler=-fPIC
)

if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Release")
  list(APPEND HPC_CUDA_FLAGS -O3)
elseif(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
  list(APPEND HPC_CUDA_FLAGS -G -g -O0)
else()
  list(APPEND HPC_CUDA_FLAGS -O2 -g --generate-line-info)
endif()

# TF32: on by default for Ampere+; disable only when maximum reproducibility needed
# (cublasMath flag; nvcc itself doesn't need explicit control)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Interface library  (header-only: all .cuh live in include/)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
add_library(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE)
target_include_directories(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
)
target_compile_options(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE
    $<$<COMPILE_LANGUAGE:CUDA>:${HPC_CUDA_FLAGS}>
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NCCL
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(HPC_ENABLE_NCCL)
  find_library(NCCL_LIBRARY nccl
      HINTS ENV NCCL_HOME /usr/local/nccl ENV CUDA_HOME /usr/local/cuda
      PATH_SUFFIXES lib lib64)
  find_path(NCCL_INCLUDE_DIR nccl.h
      HINTS ENV NCCL_HOME /usr/local/nccl /usr/local/cuda
      PATH_SUFFIXES include)

  if(NCCL_LIBRARY AND NCCL_INCLUDE_DIR)
    message(STATUS "[HPC_Optimizer] NCCL found: ${NCCL_LIBRARY}")
    target_compile_definitions(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE HPC_HAVE_NCCL)
    target_include_directories(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${NCCL_INCLUDE_DIR})
    target_link_libraries(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${NCCL_LIBRARY})
  else()
    message(WARNING "[HPC_Optimizer] NCCL requested but not found — disabling")
    set(HPC_ENABLE_NCCL OFF)
  endif()
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MPI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(HPC_ENABLE_MPI)
  find_package(MPI REQUIRED CXX)
  if(MPI_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "[HPC_Optimizer] MPI found: ${MPI_CXX_LIBRARIES}")
    target_compile_definitions(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE HPC_HAVE_MPI)
    target_include_directories(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${MPI_CXX_INCLUDE_DIRS})
    target_link_libraries(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${MPI_CXX_LIBRARIES})
  endif()
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NVTX
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(HPC_ENABLE_NVTX)
  find_library(NVTX_LIBRARY nvToolsExt
      HINTS ENV CUDA_HOME /usr/local/cuda
      PATH_SUFFIXES lib lib64)
  if(NVTX_LIBRARY)
    message(STATUS "[HPC_Optimizer] NVTX found: ${NVTX_LIBRARY}")
    target_compile_definitions(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE HPC_HAVE_NVTX)
    target_link_libraries(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${NVTX_LIBRARY})
  else()
    message(WARNING "[HPC_Optimizer] NVTX not found — profiling markers disabled")
    set(HPC_ENABLE_NVTX OFF)
  endif()
endif()

# cuBLAS — required for Tensor Parallel GEMM
find_library(CUBLAS_LIB cublas
    HINTS ENV CUDA_HOME /usr/local/cuda
    PATH_SUFFIXES lib lib64)
find_library(CUBLASLT_LIB cublasLt
    HINTS ENV CUDA_HOME /usr/local/cuda
    PATH_SUFFIXES lib lib64)
if(CUBLAS_LIB)
  message(STATUS "[HPC_Optimizer] cuBLAS found: ${CUBLAS_LIB}")
  target_compile_definitions(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE HPC_HAVE_CUBLAS)
  target_link_libraries(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${CUBLAS_LIB})
else()
  message(WARNING "[HPC_Optimizer] cuBLAS not found — Tensor Parallel GEMM will abort at runtime")
endif()
if(CUBLASLT_LIB)
  target_link_libraries(hpc_optimizer INTERFACE ${CUBLASLT_LIB})
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper macro: add an HPC executable
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
macro(hpc_add_executable name src)
  add_executable(${name} ${src})
  target_link_libraries(${name} PRIVATE hpc_optimizer)
  set_target_properties(${name} PROPERTIES
      CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION ON
      RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY   ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin
  )
endmacro()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Examples
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
hpc_add_executable(train_multigpu   examples/train_multigpu.cu)
hpc_add_executable(train_single     examples/train_single.cu)

# ZeRO-2 multi-GPU training demo (requires NCCL + MPI)
hpc_add_executable(train_zero2      examples/train_zero2.cu)
if(NOT HPC_ENABLE_NCCL)
  message(WARNING
    "[HPC_Optimizer] train_zero2 built without NCCL — "
    "will run in single-rank stub mode only.")
endif()

# ZeRO-3 + Tensor Parallel combined demo
hpc_add_executable(train_zero3_tp   examples/train_zero3_tp.cu)
if(HPC_ENABLE_NCCL AND CUBLAS_LIB)
  message(STATUS
    "[HPC_Optimizer] train_zero3_tp: ZeRO-3 + TP fully enabled.")
elseif(NOT CUBLAS_LIB)
  message(WARNING
    "[HPC_Optimizer] train_zero3_tp: cuBLAS not found — "
    "TP GEMM will abort at runtime. Link with -lcublas.")
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
enable_testing()
hpc_add_executable(test_hpc_optimizers tests/test_hpc_optimizers.cu)
hpc_add_executable(test_precision       tests/test_precision.cu)
hpc_add_executable(test_zero2           tests/test_zero2.cu)
hpc_add_executable(test_zero3           tests/test_zero3.cu)

add_test(NAME HPCOptimizerTests
         COMMAND ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_hpc_optimizers)
add_test(NAME PrecisionTests
         COMMAND ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_precision)
add_test(NAME ZeRO2Tests
         COMMAND ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_zero2)
add_test(NAME ZeRO3Tests
         COMMAND ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_zero3)

# Multi-rank ZeRO-2 integration test (only when MPI is available)
if(HPC_ENABLE_MPI AND HPC_ENABLE_NCCL)
  find_program(MPIEXEC_BIN mpirun mpiexec)
  if(MPIEXEC_BIN)
    add_test(NAME ZeRO2MultiGPU
             COMMAND ${MPIEXEC_BIN} -np 2
                     ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_zero2)
    set_tests_properties(ZeRO2MultiGPU PROPERTIES
        ENVIRONMENT "NCCL_DEBUG=WARN")
    add_test(NAME ZeRO3MultiGPU
             COMMAND ${MPIEXEC_BIN} -np 2
                     ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/test_zero3)
    set_tests_properties(ZeRO3MultiGPU PROPERTIES
        ENVIRONMENT "NCCL_DEBUG=WARN")
    add_test(NAME ZeRO3TPIntegration
             COMMAND ${MPIEXEC_BIN} -np 4
                     ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/bin/train_zero3_tp
                     --layers 2 --steps 5 --tp 2)
    set_tests_properties(ZeRO3TPIntegration PROPERTIES
        ENVIRONMENT "NCCL_DEBUG=WARN"
        TIMEOUT 120)
  endif()
endif()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Installation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
install(DIRECTORY include/
    DESTINATION include/hpc_optimizer
    FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.cuh" PATTERN "*.h")

install(TARGETS hpc_optimizer EXPORT HPCOptimizerTargets)
install(EXPORT HPCOptimizerTargets
    FILE HPCOptimizerTargets.cmake
    NAMESPACE hpc_opt::
    DESTINATION lib/cmake/HPCOptimizer)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
message(STATUS "")
message(STATUS "=== HPC Optimizer Build Configuration ===")
message(STATUS "  Build type    : ${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE}")
message(STATUS "  CUDA archs    : ${CUDA_ARCHS}")
message(STATUS "  NCCL          : ${HPC_ENABLE_NCCL}")
message(STATUS "  MPI           : ${HPC_ENABLE_MPI}")
message(STATUS "  NVTX          : ${HPC_ENABLE_NVTX}")
message(STATUS "  ZeRO-2        : enabled (hpc_zero2.cuh + hpc_zero2_optimizer.cuh)")
message(STATUS "  ZeRO-3        : enabled (hpc_zero3.cuh)")
message(STATUS "  Tensor Par.   : enabled (hpc_tensor_parallel.cuh)")
message(STATUS "  cuBLAS        : ${CUBLAS_LIB}")
message(STATUS "==========================================")
message(STATUS "")
scripts/check_regression.py:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
check_regression.py — Parse benchmark output and compare against a stored baseline.

Usage:
    python3 check_regression.py \
        --current  benchmark_results.txt \
        --baseline benchmark_baseline.txt \
        --threshold 10          # fail if any metric regressed > 10%

Exit codes:
    0  All metrics within threshold
    1  One or more metrics regressed beyond threshold
    2  Parse error (no numeric data found in files)
"""

import argparse
import re
import sys
from typing import Dict, Optional

# Regex to extract "label : value unit" or "label = value" patterns
METRIC_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?P<label>[A-Za-z][\w\s\-/]+?)"   # metric name
    r"\s*[:\|=]\s*"
    r"(?P<value>[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)"    # numeric value
    r"\s*(?P<unit>[A-Za-z/]*)",           # optional unit
    re.MULTILINE,
)

# Keywords that identify throughput / timing metrics we care about
METRIC_KEYWORDS = [
    "throughput", "bandwidth", "step", "ms/step", "GB/s", "TFlops",
    "iter", "samples/s", "tokens/s", "mean", "p95", "min", "max",
]


def parse_metrics(path: str) -> Dict[str, float]:
    """Extract numeric metrics from a benchmark log file."""
    metrics: Dict[str, float] = {}
    try:
        with open(path, "r", errors="replace") as f:
            content = f.read()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print(f"ERROR: File not found: {path}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)

    for m in METRIC_RE.finditer(content):
        label = m.group("label").strip().lower()
        # Only keep metrics related to performance
        if any(kw in label for kw in METRIC_KEYWORDS):
            key = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", label)
            try:
                metrics[key] = float(m.group("value"))
            except ValueError:
                pass

    return metrics


def compare(current: Dict[str, float], baseline: Dict[str, float],
            threshold_pct: float) -> bool:
    """
    Compare current metrics against baseline.
    Returns True if all metrics are within threshold, False if any regressed.
    """
    regressions = []
    improvements = []
    unchanged = []

    all_keys = set(current) | set(baseline)

    for key in sorted(all_keys):
        cur_val = current.get(key)
        base_val = baseline.get(key)

        if cur_val is None:
            print(f"  ⚠  MISSING in current  : {key} (baseline={base_val:.2f})")
            continue
        if base_val is None:
            print(f"  ℹ  NEW metric           : {key} = {cur_val:.2f}")
            continue

        if base_val == 0:
            pct = 0.0
        else:
            pct = (cur_val - base_val) / abs(base_val) * 100.0

        # For throughput metrics: higher is better → negative pct is regression
        # For timing metrics (ms, latency): lower is better → positive pct is regression
        is_timing = any(t in key for t in ["ms", "latency", "time", "step"])
        regressed = (pct < -threshold_pct) if not is_timing else (pct > threshold_pct)
        improved  = (pct > threshold_pct) if not is_timing else (pct < -threshold_pct)

        direction = "↓" if pct < 0 else "↑"
        marker = "✗ REGRESSION" if regressed else ("✓ improved  " if improved else "  stable    ")

        print(f"  {marker}  {key:45s}  baseline={base_val:8.2f}  current={cur_val:8.2f}  {direction}{abs(pct):.1f}%")

        if regressed:
            regressions.append((key, base_val, cur_val, pct))
        elif improved:
            improvements.append(key)
        else:
            unchanged.append(key)

    print()
    print(f"  Summary: {len(regressions)} regressions | "
          f"{len(improvements)} improvements | {len(unchanged)} unchanged")

    if regressions:
        print()
        print(f"  ✗ FAILED — {len(regressions)} metric(s) exceeded {threshold_pct}% regression threshold:")
        for key, base, cur, pct in regressions:
            print(f"      {key}: {base:.2f} → {cur:.2f}  ({pct:+.1f}%)")
        return False

    return True


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Performance regression checker")
    parser.add_argument("--current",   required=True, help="Current benchmark log")
    parser.add_argument("--baseline",  required=True, help="Baseline benchmark log")
    parser.add_argument("--threshold", type=float, default=10.0,
                        help="Regression threshold in percent (default: 10)")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    print(f"Performance Regression Check (threshold: {args.threshold}%)")
    print(f"  Current  : {args.current}")
    print(f"  Baseline : {args.baseline}")
    print()

    current  = parse_metrics(args.current)
    baseline = parse_metrics(args.baseline)

    if not current:
        print("ERROR: No metrics found in current results file.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    if not baseline:
        print("WARNING: No metrics found in baseline file — skipping regression check.")
        sys.exit(0)

    print(f"  Parsed {len(current)} metrics from current, {len(baseline)} from baseline")
    print()

    passed = compare(current, baseline, args.threshold)
    sys.exit(0 if passed else 1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
scripts/setup_runner.sh:
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# setup_runner.sh — Configure a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner on a SLURM node
#
# Run this script ONCE on the SLURM login/runner node as the CI service user.
# It installs the GitHub Actions runner agent, registers it with your repo,
# and installs a systemd unit + SLURM job-forwarding wrapper.
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - A GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) or Fine-Grained Token with
#     repo scope (Settings → Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens)
#   - CUDA toolkit, NCCL, OpenMPI already installed on compute nodes
#   - The runner node can submit SLURM jobs (squeue, sbatch, etc.)
#   - sudo / systemd access on the runner host
#
# Usage:
#   export GH_TOKEN="ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
#   export GH_REPO="your-org/hpc-optimizer"       # <owner>/<repo>
#   export RUNNER_NAME="slurm-gpu-runner-01"       # Unique runner name
#   export RUNNER_LABELS="self-hosted,slurm,gpu"   # Tags visible in ci.yml
#   bash scripts/setup_runner.sh
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GH_TOKEN="${GH_TOKEN:?Set GH_TOKEN to your GitHub PAT}"
GH_REPO="${GH_REPO:?Set GH_REPO to owner/repo}"
RUNNER_NAME="${RUNNER_NAME:-slurm-gpu-runner-$(hostname -s)}"
RUNNER_LABELS="${RUNNER_LABELS:-self-hosted,slurm,gpu,linux,x64}"
RUNNER_VERSION="${RUNNER_VERSION:-2.317.0}"
RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR="${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR:-${HOME}/actions-runner}"
RUNNER_WORK_DIR="${RUNNER_WORK_DIR:-${HOME}/actions-runner/_work}"
RUNNER_USER="${RUNNER_USER:-$(whoami)}"
SERVICE_NAME="github-runner-$(echo "${RUNNER_NAME}" | tr '/' '-')"

echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║     GitHub Actions Self-Hosted Runner Setup (SLURM)         ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "  Repo        : https://github.com/${GH_REPO}"
echo "  Runner name : ${RUNNER_NAME}"
echo "  Labels      : ${RUNNER_LABELS}"
echo "  Install dir : ${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}"
echo "  Service     : ${SERVICE_NAME}"
echo ""

# ── 1. Install system dependencies ───────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[1/7] Checking system dependencies..."

MISSING=()
for cmd in curl tar jq cmake nvcc mpirun; do
    if ! command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        MISSING+=("$cmd")
    fi
done

if [ ${#MISSING[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "  WARNING: Missing commands: ${MISSING[*]}"
    echo "  Attempting to install via apt..."
    sudo apt-get update -qq
    sudo apt-get install -y curl tar jq cmake 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "  CUDA    : $(nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | grep release | awk '{print $5}' | tr -d ',')"
echo "  CMake   : $(cmake --version | head -1)"
echo "  MPI     : $(mpirun --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo 'not found')"
echo ""

# ── 2. Download GitHub Actions runner ────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[2/7] Downloading GitHub Actions runner v${RUNNER_VERSION}..."
mkdir -p "${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}"
cd "${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}"

ARCH=$(uname -m)
case "${ARCH}" in
    x86_64)  RUNNER_ARCH="x64" ;;
    aarch64) RUNNER_ARCH="arm64" ;;
    *)        echo "Unsupported arch: ${ARCH}"; exit 1 ;;
esac

RUNNER_TARBALL="actions-runner-linux-${RUNNER_ARCH}-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
RUNNER_URL="https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${RUNNER_TARBALL}"

if [ ! -f "${RUNNER_TARBALL}" ]; then
    curl -sSL -o "${RUNNER_TARBALL}" "${RUNNER_URL}"
    tar xzf "${RUNNER_TARBALL}"
    echo "  Downloaded and extracted runner v${RUNNER_VERSION} (${RUNNER_ARCH})"
else
    echo "  Runner tarball already present — skipping download"
fi

# ── 3. Get registration token ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "[3/7] Fetching registration token from GitHub API..."
REG_TOKEN=$(curl -sSL \
    -X POST \
    -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GH_TOKEN}" \
    -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
    "https://api.github.com/repos/${GH_REPO}/actions/runners/registration-token" \
    | jq -r '.token')

if [ -z "${REG_TOKEN}" ] || [ "${REG_TOKEN}" = "null" ]; then
    echo "  ERROR: Could not obtain registration token. Check your GH_TOKEN and repo name."
    exit 1
fi
echo "  Registration token obtained ✓"

# ── 4. Configure runner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "[4/7] Configuring runner..."
cd "${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${RUNNER_WORK_DIR}"

./config.sh \
    --url "https://github.com/${GH_REPO}" \
    --token "${REG_TOKEN}" \
    --name "${RUNNER_NAME}" \
    --labels "${RUNNER_LABELS}" \
    --work "${RUNNER_WORK_DIR}" \
    --runnergroup "Default" \
    --unattended \
    --replace

echo "  Runner configured ✓"

# ── 5. Install environment shim ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# The runner executes jobs in a shell without module system. We source
# a common environment script so nvcc, mpirun, etc. are always on PATH.

echo ""
echo "[5/7] Writing environment shim..."
cat > "${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}/.env" <<'ENVEOF'
# GitHub Actions runner environment — sourced before each job
# Adjust module names to match your cluster's module system

# CUDA
CUDA_ROOT=/usr/local/cuda
export PATH="${CUDA_ROOT}/bin:${PATH}"
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${CUDA_ROOT}/lib64:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}"
export CUDA_HOME="${CUDA_ROOT}"

# NCCL (adjust to actual path)
NCCL_ROOT="${NCCL_ROOT:-/usr/local/nccl}"
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${NCCL_ROOT}/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
export NCCL_HOME="${NCCL_ROOT}"

# OpenMPI (adjust to actual path)
MPI_ROOT="${MPI_ROOT:-/usr/local/openmpi}"
export PATH="${MPI_ROOT}/bin:${PATH}"
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${MPI_ROOT}/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"

# CMake (if installed outside system path)
# export PATH="/opt/cmake/bin:${PATH}"

# SLURM environment (already set by host, but ensure visible)
export SLURM_CONF="${SLURM_CONF:-/etc/slurm/slurm.conf}"
ENVEOF

echo "  Environment shim written to ${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}/.env"

# ── 6. Install systemd service ────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "[6/7] Installing systemd service..."

SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"

sudo tee "${SERVICE_FILE}" > /dev/null <<SVCEOF
[Unit]
Description=GitHub Actions Runner (${RUNNER_NAME})
After=network-online.target slurmctld.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${RUNNER_USER}
WorkingDirectory=${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}
EnvironmentFile=${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}/.env
ExecStart=${RUNNER_INSTALL_DIR}/run.sh
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
KillMode=process
KillSignal=SIGTERM
TimeoutStopSec=5min

# Resource limits — runner process itself is lightweight; GPU jobs run via SLURM
LimitNOFILE=65536
LimitNPROC=4096

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
SVCEOF

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}"
sudo systemctl start "${SERVICE_NAME}"

sleep 2
STATUS=$(sudo systemctl is-active "${SERVICE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
echo "  Service ${SERVICE_NAME}: ${STATUS}"

# ── 7. Validate ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "[7/7] Validating runner registration..."

RUNNER_STATUS=$(curl -sSL \
    -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GH_TOKEN}" \
    -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
    "https://api.github.com/repos/${GH_REPO}/actions/runners" \
    | jq -r --arg name "${RUNNER_NAME}" '.runners[] | select(.name==$name) | .status')

if [ "${RUNNER_STATUS}" = "online" ]; then
    echo "  Runner '${RUNNER_NAME}' is ONLINE ✓"
else
    echo "  Runner '${RUNNER_NAME}' status: ${RUNNER_STATUS:-not found}"
    echo "  Check logs: sudo journalctl -u ${SERVICE_NAME} -f"
fi

echo ""
echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║                    Setup Complete!                          ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "  Useful commands:"
echo "    sudo systemctl status ${SERVICE_NAME}"
echo "    sudo journalctl -u ${SERVICE_NAME} -f"
echo "    sudo systemctl restart ${SERVICE_NAME}"
echo ""
echo "  To add more GPU labels (e.g. multi-gpu), re-run with:"
echo "    RUNNER_LABELS='self-hosted,slurm,gpu,multi-gpu' bash $0"
echo ""
echo "  Runner work dir: ${RUNNER_WORK_DIR}"
echo "  Test logs dir  : ${RUNNER_WORK_DIR}/../logs"
scripts/slurm_test.sh:
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# slurm_test.sh — SLURM sbatch script for running HPC Optimizer test suite
#
# Usage:
#   sbatch slurm_test.sh [--build-type Release|Debug] [--suite all|<name>]
#
# Submit from repo root:
#   sbatch scripts/slurm_test.sh
#   sbatch scripts/slurm_test.sh --suite test_zero3
#   sbatch --export=BUILD_TYPE=Debug scripts/slurm_test.sh
# =============================================================================

#SBATCH --job-name=hpc-optimizer-tests
#SBATCH --partition=gpu
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=16
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:a100:1              # Request 1× A100 for single-GPU tests
#SBATCH --mem=64G
#SBATCH --time=01:30:00
#SBATCH --output=logs/slurm_test_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=logs/slurm_test_%j.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=${SLURM_NOTIFY_EMAIL:-""}

# ── Configuration (override via --export or environment) ──────────────────────
BUILD_TYPE="${BUILD_TYPE:-Release}"
CUDA_ARCH="${CUDA_ARCH:-80}"           # 70=V100, 80=A100, 90=H100
TEST_SUITE="${TEST_SUITE:-all}"        # all | test_hpc_optimizers | test_precision | test_zero2 | test_zero3
ENABLE_NCCL="${ENABLE_NCCL:-ON}"
ENABLE_MPI="${ENABLE_MPI:-ON}"
ENABLE_NVTX="${ENABLE_NVTX:-ON}"
TEST_TIMEOUT="${TEST_TIMEOUT:-300}"    # Per-test timeout in seconds
BUILD_DIR="build_slurm_${BUILD_TYPE}_sm${CUDA_ARCH}"
LOG_DIR="logs"
RESULTS_DIR="test_results"

# ── Derived ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
JUNIT_XML="${RESULTS_DIR}/junit_${TEST_SUITE}_${TIMESTAMP}_job${SLURM_JOB_ID}.xml"

# ── Setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail
cd "${REPO_ROOT}"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}" "${RESULTS_DIR}"

echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║          HPC Optimizer — SLURM Test Runner                  ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "  Job ID       : ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "  Node         : ${SLURMD_NODENAME}"
echo "  Build type   : ${BUILD_TYPE}"
echo "  CUDA arch    : sm_${CUDA_ARCH}"
echo "  Test suite   : ${TEST_SUITE}"
echo "  Build dir    : ${BUILD_DIR}"
echo "  Timestamp    : ${TIMESTAMP}"
echo ""

# ── Module loads (adjust to your cluster's module system) ─────────────────────
echo "[1/5] Loading modules..."
module purge 2>/dev/null || true

# Try common CUDA module names (adjust for your cluster)
for cuda_mod in "cuda/12.2" "cuda/12.0" "cuda/11.8" "cuda"; do
    if module load "${cuda_mod}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Loaded: ${cuda_mod}"
        break
    fi
done

# NCCL
for nccl_mod in "nccl/2.18" "nccl/2.16" "nccl"; do
    if module load "${nccl_mod}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Loaded: ${nccl_mod}"
        break
    fi
done

# MPI
for mpi_mod in "openmpi/4.1" "openmpi/4.0" "mpich/4.0"; do
    if module load "${mpi_mod}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Loaded: ${mpi_mod}"
        break
    fi
done

# CMake
for cmake_mod in "cmake/3.27" "cmake/3.25" "cmake/3.20" "cmake"; do
    if module load "${cmake_mod}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Loaded: ${cmake_mod}"
        break
    fi
done

echo ""
nvcc --version | head -1
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,driver_version --format=csv,noheader | head -1
echo ""

# ── GPU Environment ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Use the GPUs allocated by SLURM
if [ -n "${SLURM_JOB_GPUS:-}" ]; then
    export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="${SLURM_JOB_GPUS}"
elif [ -n "${SLURM_STEP_GPUS:-}" ]; then
    export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="${SLURM_STEP_GPUS}"
fi
export CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER=PCI_BUS_ID
echo "  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES = ${CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES:-auto}"
echo ""

# ── Configure ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[2/5] Configuring CMake..."
cmake -B "${BUILD_DIR}" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="${BUILD_TYPE}" \
    -DCUDA_ARCHS="${CUDA_ARCH}" \
    -DHPC_ENABLE_NCCL="${ENABLE_NCCL}" \
    -DHPC_ENABLE_MPI="${ENABLE_MPI}" \
    -DHPC_ENABLE_NVTX="${ENABLE_NVTX}" \
    -DCMAKE_CUDA_FLAGS="-Xptxas -v" \
    2>&1 | tee "${LOG_DIR}/cmake_config_${TIMESTAMP}.log"

echo ""

# ── Build ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[3/5] Building (${BUILD_TYPE}, sm_${CUDA_ARCH})..."
TIME_BUILD_START=$(date +%s)
cmake --build "${BUILD_DIR}" --parallel "${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK:-8}" \
    2>&1 | tee "${LOG_DIR}/cmake_build_${TIMESTAMP}.log"
TIME_BUILD_END=$(date +%s)
echo "  Build completed in $((TIME_BUILD_END - TIME_BUILD_START))s"
echo ""

# ── Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[4/5] Running tests..."
TIME_TEST_START=$(date +%s)

cd "${BUILD_DIR}"

# Build CTest filter
if [ "${TEST_SUITE}" = "all" ]; then
    CTEST_FILTER=""
    echo "  Running all 48 tests across 4 suites"
else
    CTEST_FILTER="-R ${TEST_SUITE}"
    echo "  Running suite: ${TEST_SUITE}"
fi

# Run CTest with JUnit output
ctest \
    ${CTEST_FILTER} \
    --output-on-failure \
    --timeout "${TEST_TIMEOUT}" \
    --output-junit "../${JUNIT_XML}" \
    --parallel 1 \
    -V 2>&1 | tee "../${LOG_DIR}/ctest_${TIMESTAMP}.log"

CTEST_EXIT=$?
cd "${REPO_ROOT}"

TIME_TEST_END=$(date +%s)
echo ""
echo "  Test run completed in $((TIME_TEST_END - TIME_TEST_START))s"
echo "  JUnit XML: ${JUNIT_XML}"
echo ""

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "[5/5] Summary"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

if [ -f "${JUNIT_XML}" ]; then
    # Parse JUnit XML for quick summary
    TOTAL=$(grep -o 'tests="[0-9]*"' "${JUNIT_XML}" | grep -o '[0-9]*' | head -1 || echo "?")
    FAILURES=$(grep -o 'failures="[0-9]*"' "${JUNIT_XML}" | grep -o '[0-9]*' | head -1 || echo "?")
    ERRORS=$(grep -o 'errors="[0-9]*"' "${JUNIT_XML}" | grep -o '[0-9]*' | head -1 || echo "?")
    SKIPPED=$(grep -o 'skipped="[0-9]*"' "${JUNIT_XML}" | grep -o '[0-9]*' | head -1 || echo "0")
    echo "  Total    : ${TOTAL}"
    echo "  Failures : ${FAILURES}"
    echo "  Errors   : ${ERRORS}"
    echo "  Skipped  : ${SKIPPED}"
fi

echo ""
if [ ${CTEST_EXIT} -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ All tests PASSED"
else
    echo "  ✗ Some tests FAILED (CTest exit: ${CTEST_EXIT})"
fi
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

# ── Multi-GPU extension: submit follow-up job if 1+ GPU available ─────────────
if [ "${RUN_MULTIGPU:-OFF}" = "ON" ]; then
    GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader | wc -l)
    if [ "${GPU_COUNT}" -ge 2 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo "Submitting multi-GPU follow-up job (${GPU_COUNT} GPUs available)..."
        sbatch \
            --gres=gpu:a100:2 \
            --export=ALL,BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}",TEST_SUITE="multigpu" \
            scripts/slurm_multigpu_test.sh
    fi
fi

exit ${CTEST_EXIT}
